title: Delicate Velvety Japanese Eggs Tamagoyaki
description: A traditional Japanese dish featuring tender eggs, tangy ginger paste, and a hint of render-fat, perfect for a kid-friendly meal. This recipe is shojin-friendly and made in the style of Kansai cuisine.
instructions: Crack the eggs into a bowl and whisk them gently to avoid creating air pockets.
Add the ginger paste, soy sauce, sake, mirin, and salt to the eggs and whisk until well combined.
Heat the render-fat in a small non-stick pan over medium heat.
Pour a small amount of the egg mixture into the pan and tilt to evenly coat the bottom.
Use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the egg towards the center.
Repeat the process of pouring the egg mixture and folding the edges until all the mixture is used up.
Remove the tamagoyaki from the pan and let it cool on a plate.
Slice the tamagoyaki into small pieces and serve warm.
Traditionally, tamagoyaki is served as a side dish or used as an ingredient in bento boxes.
To make ahead, cook the tamagoyaki and store it in the refrigerator for up to a day.
Reheat the tamagoyaki in the microwave or in a pan with a small amount of water before serving.
